title: Per-entry truncationReason over-stamped from section/result-level aggregates; complete breadcrumbs flagged truncated

findingDetails.evidence: finalize() (412-421) stamps EVERY entry in a section with 'deadline'/'trace_limit' whenever deadlineExceeded or omittedWhyIncluded>0, including entries captured fully before the cap. stampContextTraceTruncation (282-295), invoked at 216-220 when the textBrief string is truncated, stamps why_included entries across ALL sections with 'budget' even though why_included is structured data never truncated by the token budget.
findingDetails.why: truncationReason is a per-entry field semantically meaning 'this chain was cut short', but it is driven by result/section-level partiality. A consumer reading truncationReason!==null on a complete, accurate breadcrumb concludes the provenance is incomplete (e.g. when only OTHER files hit CONTEXT_WHY_INCLUDED_LIMIT, or only the human-readable textBrief was sliced). Direct inaccuracy in the provenance artifact under review.
findingDetails.recommendation: Stamp truncationReason only on entries actually affected: 'trace_limit' only when this file was the one omitted (or attach a section-level flag instead), 'deadline' only on entries recorded after the deadline boundary, and do NOT propagate textBrief 'budget' truncation onto why_included (it is returned in full via graphContext). Alternatively rename the field to a section/result-level partial flag to match its true granularity.

title: ambiguous hard-coded false for all non-anchor breadcrumb entries regardless of edge confidence/evidenceClass

findingDetails.evidence: recordWhyIncluded sets ambiguous:false unconditionally (line 482). Test code-graph-context-handler.vitest.ts:398 asserts ambiguous:false for an edge with evidenceClass:'INFERRED', detectorProvenance:'heuristic', confidence:0.8. Anchor entries by contrast derive ambiguous from anchor.resolution!=='exact' (404).
findingDetails.why: The provenance breadcrumb cannot signal that a neighbor was included via a heuristic/inferred edge versus a resolved one. If 'ambiguous' is meant to flag uncertain inclusion, a heuristic INFERRED edge should not report ambiguous:false. Defensible only if the field strictly means node-identity (not edge) ambiguity, which is undocumented.
findingDetails.recommendation: Derive ambiguous for neighbor entries from edge evidence (e.g. evidenceClass==='INFERRED' or confidence below a threshold), or document that 'ambiguous' refers solely to anchor-resolution identity and add a separate edge-reliability signal.

title: Breadcrumb keeps only the first edge when a file is reachable via multiple depth-1 edges

findingDetails.evidence: recordWhyIncluded returns early when previous && previous.depth <= input.depth (459). All neighbor records use depth:1, so a file reached by two depth-1 edges (e.g. anchor CALLS X.foo and IMPORTS X.bar) keeps only the first edge; edgeChain is always a single-element array and the second reason is dropped.
findingDetails.why: For a file included for multiple distinct reasons, the provenance reports only one, under-representing why it was pulled in. edgeChain's array shape implies multiple steps but never holds more than one entry.
findingDetails.recommendation: On a same-depth collision, append the additional edge to edgeChain (or keep the highest-confidence edge) instead of discarding; or document that the breadcrumb is a single representative path.

findingDetails.evidence: expandAnchor is called only at line 198, which executes only after buildContext's `if (!anchor.symbolId)` block (174-196) has `continue`d past every file anchor. Thus anchor.symbolId is always truthy on entry, and the `if (!anchor.symbolId)` branch at 487-499 (which builds a why_included via spread of whyIncludedByFile values rather than the finalize() path) is dead.
findingDetails.why: Dead code carries a second, divergent why_included construction path (raw spread, no truncationReason normalization) that could mislead future maintainers into thinking file anchors flow through expandAnchor.
findingDetails.recommendation: Remove lines 487-499, or add an assertion/comment that expandAnchor is symbol-anchor-only since buildContext pre-handles file anchors.
